events.title: Four people, one poster
events.text: Your group project has plenty of opinions and no actual plan. The deadline is getting close.

choices.label: Make a plan and delegate
choices.outcome: The project comes together, though you do more than your fair share.

choices.label: Get everyone heard
choices.outcome: It takes longer, but the final work belongs to the whole group.

choices.label: Do only your section
choices.outcome: Your part is solid. The poster as a whole has… character.

events.title: The crowded exam calendar
events.text: Three important tests have landed in the same week, along with everything else in your life.

choices.label: Build a realistic study plan
choices.outcome: The plan survives contact with reality surprisingly well.

choices.label: Cram through the nights
choices.outcome: You remember enough answers, and almost none of the week itself.

choices.label: Do what you can and rest
choices.outcome: The scores are mixed, but you still feel like yourself afterward.

events.title: Tables full of possibilities
events.text: The club fair fills the hall with handmade signs, demonstrations, and enthusiastic invitations.

choices.label: Try the speaking club
choices.outcome: You discover that a good question can be stronger than a loud answer.

choices.label: Join the makers' table
choices.outcome: By the end of the hour, your hands are dusty and your head is full of ideas.

choices.label: Help with the garden group
choices.outcome: The work is quiet, steady, and oddly satisfying.

events.title: A note after class
events.text: A teacher asks you to stay behind—not for trouble, but because they think you could aim higher.

choices.label: Take on the extra challenge
choices.outcome: The work is difficult in exactly the right way.

choices.label: Protect your free time
choices.outcome: You appreciate the confidence, but choose a pace you can sustain.

events.title: The date you circled wrong
events.text: A major assignment was due yesterday, not next week. Your nearly empty document confirms the problem.

choices.label: Own the mistake and ask for time
choices.outcome: You lose some marks, but gain a second chance.

choices.label: Submit a frantic first draft
choices.outcome: It has a beginning, an ending, and several paragraphs that disagree with each other.

choices.label: Invent a technical disaster
choices.outcome: The story works, but now you need to remember every detail of it.

events.title: The tuition gap
events.text: A grant covers less than expected, leaving a sharp hole in this year's study budget.

choices.label: Take extra paid shifts
choices.outcome: Your calendar becomes a puzzle with very little blank space.

choices.label: Use a student loan
choices.outcome: The immediate pressure eases, and future-you receives an IOU.

choices.label: Appeal the funding decision
choices.outcome: A careful application earns a small additional award.

events.title: The fault no one can find
events.text: A training rig keeps failing in an unusual way. Even the instructor pauses to think.

choices.label: Test one possibility at a time
choices.outcome: Your patient process exposes a tiny, hidden fault.

choices.label: Pull the group together
choices.outcome: Different observations finally connect into an answer.

choices.label: Try the most likely fix
choices.outcome: It works briefly, which is not quite the same as working.

events.title: The room beyond your notes
events.text: You must present your work to a room full of people who may ask difficult questions.

choices.label: Practice until the opening feels natural
choices.outcome: The questions still surprise you, but you know the material well enough to adapt.

choices.label: Trust yourself in the moment
choices.outcome: A few sentences wander, yet your enthusiasm carries the room.

choices.label: Ask to submit a recording
choices.outcome: The recording is clear, though the avoided room lingers in your thoughts.
